etymology.description: Four parallel lines -> represents the number four before the Qin Dynasty
etymology.interpretation: The character 亖 (sǐ) was an early representation of the number four, consisting of four horizontal lines. It was used before the standardized writing reforms of the Qin Dynasty, which replaced it with 四 (sì) to prevent confusion with 二 (èr, 'two') and 三 (sān, 'three') in vertical text.
